description: General growth habits and spreading forms.
plants: Ascending Plant
Stems that start low and flat before curving upward, as if changing their mind about staying on the ground.
Clump-forming Plant
A tight cluster of stems rising together from the same spot, growing outward a little more each year.
Columnar Plant
A narrow green pillar rising straight up, taking almost no space on the ground but visible from a distance.
Cushion Plant
A tight dome pressed close to the earth, trapping warmth inside itself to survive the cold.
Erect Plant
Stems that grow straight up without leaning or sprawling, standing at attention in any weather.
Geophyte
Disappears entirely above ground when its season ends, waiting as a bulb in the dark soil until the time is right.
Groundcover
A low carpet of green spreading across bare earth, filling every gap until the ground itself disappears.
Mat-forming Plant
Stems woven together into a flat, tight mat that lies across the ground like a living rug.
Mound-forming Plant
A rounded heap of leaves and stems that looks almost deliberate, like someone shaped it on purpose.
Prostrate Plant
Branches pressed flat against the ground, only the tips lifting up, as if crawling forward on its belly.
Rhizomatous Plant
Spreads underground through hidden stems, surfacing in new spots so you never quite know where it will appear next.
Rosette Plant
Leaves fanning out from a single center point like a green star pressed flat against the earth.
Stoloniferous Plant
Sends runners across the surface, each one rooting down to start a new copy of itself.
Suckering Plant
New shoots push up from the roots at a distance, so what looks like many plants is really one connected underground.
Tuberous Plant
Swollen roots packed with starch hidden beneath the soil, a buried pantry waiting for the next growing season.
Virgate Plant
Long slender shoots rising straight and unbranched, swaying like wands in the wind.
Weeping Plant
Branches that curve and hang downward, swaying like curtains and hiding whatever is underneath.
Woody Cushion
A hard woody dome shaped by relentless alpine wind into something that looks carved from the mountainside.
